data.narrative:
World Labs, a Series B AI & Software company founded by Fei-Fei Li, Justin Johnson, Christoph Lassner, and Ben Mildenhall, is valued at $5.0B post-money following $1.23B+ in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises one public comparable (Palantir) and eight private-stage companies, with four same-sector, same-stage peers directly informing valuation analysis.

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement to the $5.0B last post-money valuation, implying $9.5B. Peer-median analysis across four directly comparable AI & Software Series B companies—Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M)—yields a median valuation of $3.3B, with a range spanning $500M to $32.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneous capital intensity and go-to-market dynamics within frontier AI.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $2.8B (low) to $10.9B (high), with a median of $6.4B. World Labs' current $5.0B valuation sits below the triangulated median, positioning it in the lower-middle quartile of the implied range. Key uncertainties include differentiation within the peer set—particularly the outsized $32.0B Safe Superintelligence valuation—and the limited public comparables available for this nascent sector.
